data.title: Microservices
data.description: Google uses a microservices architecture allowing different services to communicate over a network through HTTP/HTTPS or other protocols.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Google employs advanced load balancing to distribute traffic across multiple servers and data centers.
data.technical_spec: Global load balancers, Anycast DNS, HTTP/2 support

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Globally distributed data centers ensure redundancy and reduce latency for users across the world.
data.technical_spec: Tier 3 and 4 data centers, fiber-optic connectivity, renewable energy powered

data.title: Containerization
data.description: Applications are containerized to ensure consistent environments and efficient resource utilization.
data.technical_spec: Docker containers, Borg/Omega for cluster management

data.title: Scalability
data.description: Scalable infrastructure to handle billions of search queries and process vast amounts of data globally.
data.technical_spec: Automatic scaling with Kubernetes, custom VMs, horizontally distributed architecture
